title: Power BI REST API — Import, Refresh, Credentials, Workspace Management, Python Patterns

confirmed_facts.fact: Power BI REST API base URL: https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/. Workspace-scoped endpoints use /groups/{workspaceId}/. Auth scope: https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default

Service principal auth requires: (1) Entra ID app registration with client secret, (2) Power BI Admin Portal > 'Allow service principals to use Power BI APIs' enabled for a security group, (3) SP added to target workspace as Member or Admin.

Import PBIX: POST /groups/{workspaceId}/imports?datasetDisplayName={name}&nameConflict=Overwrite — multipart/form-data. Returns 202, poll GET /imports/{importId} until importState = Succeeded|Failed. Max 1GB via direct upload.

Trigger refresh: POST /groups/{workspaceId}/datasets/{datasetId}/refreshes with empty JSON body. Returns 202. Poll GET /datasets/{datasetId}/refreshes for status. Limits: 8/day (Pro), 48/day (Premium).

Service principals CANNOT authenticate to SharePoint Online for scheduled refresh. Dataset credentials for SharePoint sources must use a licensed M365 user with OAuth2 configured in Power BI Service settings.
Workspace hierarchy: workspace → dataset (semantic model) → report (visualizations) → dashboard (pinned tiles). Roles: Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer.

PBIX file is a ZIP archive containing: DataModel (VertiPaq binary), Report/Layout (JSON), Connections (JSON with M expressions), SecurityBindings (RLS definitions), Version. DataModel is not human-readable. Direct programmatic creation of .pbix is NOT supported.

Scheduled refresh: PATCH /datasets/{datasetId}/refreshSchedule. Days array: ["Monday","Tuesday",...]. Times in HH:MM. Inactivity pause: auto-paused after 2 months of no user activity on reports/dashboards.
Export report: POST /reports/{reportId}/ExportTo with format (PDF, PPTX, PNG, XLSX). Async — poll GET /reports/{reportId}/exports/{exportId}, then download file.

implementation_notes:
Complete Python: authenticate, import PBIX, trigger refresh:
```python
import requests
import time

def get_pbi_token(tenant_id, client_id, client_secret):
    url = f'https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token'
    resp = requests.post(url, data={
        'grant_type': 'client_credentials',
        'client_id': client_id,
        'client_secret': client_secret,
        'scope': 'https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default',
    })
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()['access_token']

def import_pbix(workspace_id, name, pbix_path, token):
    url = (f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/imports'
           f'?datasetDisplayName={name}&nameConflict=Overwrite')
    with open(pbix_path, 'rb') as f:
        resp = requests.post(url,
            headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'},
            files={'file': (f'{name}.pbix', f, 'application/octet-stream')})
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()['id']

def wait_import(workspace_id, import_id, token, timeout=300):
    url = f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/imports/{import_id}'
    start = time.time()
    while time.time() - start < timeout:
        resp = requests.get(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
        resp.raise_for_status()
        state = resp.json().get('importState')
        if state == 'Succeeded':
            return resp.json()
        if state == 'Failed':
            raise RuntimeError(f'Import failed: {resp.json()}')
        time.sleep(5)
    raise TimeoutError('Import timed out')

def trigger_refresh(workspace_id, dataset_id, token):
    url = f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/refreshes'
    resp = requests.post(url,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        json={})
    resp.raise_for_status()  # 202 Accepted
```

Check refresh status:
```python
def get_refresh_status(workspace_id, dataset_id, token):
    url = f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/refreshes'
    resp = requests.get(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
    resp.raise_for_status()
    refreshes = resp.json().get('value', [])
    if refreshes:
        latest = refreshes[0]
        return latest.get('status'), latest.get('startTime'), latest.get('endTime')
    return None, None, None
```

Update dataset parameters (e.g. change SharePoint site URL without re-publishing):
```python
def update_parameters(workspace_id, dataset_id, token, params):
    url = f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/Default.UpdateParameters'
    body = {'updateDetails': [{'name': k, 'newValue': v} for k, v in params.items()]}
    resp = requests.post(url,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        json=body)
    resp.raise_for_status()
```

Configure scheduled refresh:
```python
def set_refresh_schedule(workspace_id, dataset_id, token, days, times, timezone='UTC'):
    url = f'https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/refreshSchedule'
    body = {'value': {
        'enabled': True,
        'notifyOption': 'MailOnFailure',
        'days': days,           # ['Monday', 'Wednesday', 'Friday']
        'times': times,         # ['06:00', '18:00']
        'localTimeZoneId': timezone,
    }}
    resp = requests.patch(url,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        json=body)
    resp.raise_for_status()
```

Monthly automation pattern:
1. Upload new CSV to SharePoint (separate process or Logic App)
2. Trigger Power BI refresh via POST /datasets/{id}/refreshes
3. Poll GET /datasets/{id}/refreshes until status = 'Completed'
4. Export report to PDF: POST /reports/{id}/ExportTo
5. Poll GET /reports/{id}/exports/{exportId} until status = 'Succeeded'
6. Download: GET /reports/{id}/exports/{exportId}/file
7. Send PDF via email or upload to SharePoint
caveats: nameConflict=Overwrite replaces both dataset AND report — existing report bookmarks and subscriptions may be lost
Max PBIX upload via direct POST is 1GB — for 1-10GB use Create Temporary Upload Location first
Refresh limits are strict: 8/day Pro, 48/day Premium — exceeding returns 400 error
Scheduled refresh auto-pauses after 2 months of no user activity on reports/dashboards
Service principals cannot set SharePoint Online OAuth credentials — must use a licensed user account
Import is async (202) — must poll for completion before triggering refresh or reading dataset ID
Power BI API permissions (Dataset.ReadWrite.All, etc.) must be set in Entra ID AND the SP must be added to the workspace
